data_coverage_summary: Demo applications, libraries and SDKs as well as useful connectivity tools which are maintained and released as open source software. Published API scope is centered on SDK-driven ingestion and low-code data flow integration.
buyer_guidance: Use these SDK and flow-node surfaces when the integration needs developer-side ingestion or orchestration tooling rather than a turnkey business API.
overview: Demo applications, libraries and SDKs as well as useful connectivity tools which are maintained and released as open source software. These open-source tools and libraries exist to push industrial data into Siemens IoT platforms and wire connectivity into Node.js or Node-RED flows. Asset data is Supported. Telemetry data is Supported. Writeback is Supported.

available_data.description: Published API scope is centered on SDK-driven ingestion and low-code data flow integration.

ingest_considerations: This surface includes telemetry or event-oriented payloads that should be modeled for time-based ingestion or monitoring workflows.
The published surface includes mutation or submission capabilities, so integrations should enforce explicit governance around which upstream systems are allowed to write.
The evidence for this record comes from the published Siemens overview and product summary; the public artifact did not expose a simple path list during generation.

source_evidence.evidence_notes: Classification is based on the official Siemens product overview and the published spec artifact for MindConnect-NodeJS.
